steps.title: Use the same-line condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has infinitely many solutions only when both equations describe the same line.

steps.title: Scale the first equation
steps.content:
Make the coefficient of $y$ equal to $1$ by multiplying the first equation by $4$:
$$4(\frac{2}{5}x+\frac{1}{4}y)=4(\frac{3}{8})$$
$$\frac{8}{5}x+y=\frac{3}{2}$$

steps.title: Match the coefficient
steps.content: The second equation is $ax+y=c$. To describe the same line, its coefficient of $x$ must match $\frac{8}{5}$. The answer is D.
